channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: /smg/ - stock market genera — 264 replies — 1056.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
/smg/ - stock market genera

Open thread • Replies: 264 • Updated: 2026-06-30 16:05 UTC

OP

Abenomics edition

>Educational sites:
https://www.investopedia.com

>Financial TV Streams:
https://www.newslive.com/american/cnbc.html
https://www.livestreamy.net/bloomberg/

>Charts:
https://www.tradingview.com
https://www.finscreener.com
https://www.portfoliovisualizer.com

>Screeners:
https://finviz.com/
https://etfdb.com/

>Gambling:
https://www.optionsplaybook.com/options-introduction/
https://www.optionsprofitcalculator.com
https://optionstrat.com/
https://www.optionistics.com/quotes/option-prices

>Pre-Market and Live data:
https://www.investing.com/indices/indices-futures
https://finance.yahoo.com/

>Calendars:
https://www.marketwatch.com/economy-politics/calendar
https://www.earningswhispers.com/calendar
https://www.cmegroup.com/trading/interest-rates/countdown-to-fomc.html
https://www.mortgagenewsdaily.com/data/building-permits

>Boomer Investing 101:
https://www.bogleheads.org/wiki/Getting_started
https://www.sec.gov/search-filings

>Misc:
https://www.financialjuice.com/home
https://finance.yahoo.com/trending-tickers
https://market24hclock.com/
https://www.dividendchannel.com/drip-returns-calculator
https://brokerchooser.com/
https://www.chathamfinancial.com/technology/us-market-rates

>>62429521

Early replies (first 3)

62430417
I am irrationally exuberant and it feels good

62430419
BUY BUY BUY
(rolls royce)

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62430853
In 2-3 months everyone is going to say Microsoft was the most obvious buy in history

62430854
>>62430799
>Micron Technology Inc 25.99%
>Samsung Electronics Co 24.63%
>SK hynix 24.21%
It's mostly just gook ram, not semis as a whole. My actual semis ETF is 3% up.

62430855
>>62430844
well from the chart it touched the double low of around 365-368 after a massive spike down, and then had a massive spike up. standard stuff no?

62430857
>>62430812
I just looked up the channel due to nostalgia, I didn't know Big Lenny had passed away, damn that's sad. Couldn't find anything on Jay.

>>62430816
I used to binge watch Delray all the time when I first got into lifting at 17. 29 now and still trying to be a sick freak. I've slowed down a little because life gets busier when you get older, but damn time flies.

62430858
Do you cup your balls when you pee?

62430860
>>62430855
I guess TA traders could be the reason. Really no news at all otherwise

62430861
>>62430842

Im a main character. Most people here are NPCs or bots or 2 digit IQ

62430862
Asking again, should I sell AMD at 580? Help a retard out.

62430864
>>62430858
No I pay someone to do it for me

62430866
>>62430858
If there is no one else around to help, then yes I do it myself

62430867
>>62430862
No, you hold until at least Q1 of next year.

62430868
>>62430858
yes that's all I can hold down there since my penis inverted

62430871
>>62430861
>I'm a main character
>"A" main character, not "THE" main character
you believe in yourself but still got a good portion of humility. ygmi

62430872
>>62430858
No, I drop my pants all the way down and sit on the toilet seat. I also use a bidet to hose my asshole clean anyway.

62430876
Sharting

62430877
>>62430746

>OIL
never selling

62430880
>>62430867
I mean sell and rebuy after a dump.

62430884
>>62430854
Which semis ETF are you in?

62430885
>>62430880
Well, good luck trying to time the market.

62430888
>Saylor getting ready to refinance

62430889
>head and shoulders
im ready.

62430890
SPCX bros how we feeling?

62430892
>>62430853

>Burry bought $700 MSFT calls
>Bill Ackman bought MSFT
>Jeremy Grantham bought MSFT
>Cliff Asness bought MSFT

I'm feeling good

62430896
>>62430885
True dat, homie

62430898
What ETF can you buy at ATH's and not worry about the AI bubble crash?

62430899
>>62430884
ShakeMyHead
But it's European accumulating one with different holdings rather than the distributing one.

It's got a decent chunk of AMD and INTC, which I do like. I also am exposed to Gooks and Chinks with Prime Emerging markets and South East Asian ETF-
https://www.londonstockexchange.com/stock/BAKA/van-eck-global/company-page

62430901
>>62430892
>mfw I am smarter than famous asset managers for seeing a value trap

62430904
There's no way we keep pumping into the weekend, right?

62430905
>>62430901

I shilled it at the pico bottom and its not a trap. cya at 500 faggot

62430907
yo kevin pump it again!
just for funsies!

item.title: VOO and Chill — 160 replies — 640.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
VOO and Chill

Open thread • Replies: 160 • Updated: 2026-06-30 16:05 UTC

OP

The more I look into investing and compare the historical returns of certain ETFs / individual stocks it seems like the whole philosophy of “VOO and Chill” is equal parts really smart and retarded
It’s like a no brainer for money but even now as long as the market is in a growth period shouldn’t you at least do QQQ?
It seems so simple that’d you miss a lot of upside but the returns are honestly not bad and extremely consistent
How accurate is this assessment? It’s both so simple and guaranteed that everyone should it but also too safe so you end up missing out on more upside
I’m talking 5-20 years investing here not trading

Early replies (first 3)

62400515
Boomer funds are gay and lame

62400554
>>62400515
What qualifies as one
Why
What crypto do you want to shill

62400560
boglehead method is the tried and tested way, buy the market with an index fund that has a low fee, invest monthly, do it consistently for decades and you WILL be rich
but biz/anons don't care about being rich when they're older, they want to be rich NOW
greed is a deadly sin

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62423192
Are you a bad enough dude to hold and make it?

62423236
>>62423192
Is this a backtest or actual holdings in real time, I dont recognize the site UI

62423245
>>62423192
for reference, this is what Im rockin...

62423267
40/40/20 VDY/XEI/XEQT is extremely comfy for any Canadians reading this thread

62423719
>>62422917
retard he is telling you to risk adjust your allocations as you get older, when you're 60 it's more about capital preservation than getting high returns

62423769
this thread is getting dumber by the day

62423809
Using a reversion to the mean strategy on indexes, I managed to get a 17% CAGR over 54 years only trading indexes/ETFs (where data permits) on backtesting, significantly beating each individual index . More enquiry is warranted but I think "VOO and chill" can be improved upon and optimized.

62423821
>>62423719
he mentioned rn

62423875
>>62400636
It actually is something tho. $150 a month becomes 170k at 7% real returns for 30 years. This gives you an extra $570/mo in retirement.

Is that a LOT? Fuck no. But we’re talking poorfag standards where social security is the only thing you have. An extra 500-600 a month is a significant increase over base SS for a poorfag especially if you move to an ultra low cost of living area.

62423880
The reason i keep bringing up DRAM and other return stuff is because it's on my mind to go into that stuff now, sell, and then increase my VOO position even further - not to infinitely chase returns btw

62424100
>>62423880
I'm putting 20k into micron tomorrow. I feel like it's not to late for this to just keep going up. Its not going to 10x but 4-5, yeah...

62424179
>>62424100
why only micron, why not dram etf

62424607
>>62400636
>The problem is we have people starting at age 35 with like 150 a month thinking it's going to somehow mean something.
I'm in this and doing VT. Maybe I'll be ok probably not. Can do more than $150 though

62424664
I seriously cannot decide between just not thinking and doing broad ETFs and trying to chase this AI thing

62426022
>>62423236
>>62423245
It's a mostly unserious backtest, site was posted earlier >>62419980

>>62423769
Why not have some fun and make the most unhinged etf port possible??

62426103
>>62426022
Interesting thanks

62426138
I have been degenerately long-only various semiconductors since the april bottom and yet on 90% of days, I've underperformed soxl on the way up and outperformed it on the way down. After doing all my research and picking the right names and calculating the right options to buy, I should have thrown all my notes in the trash and just bought soxl. Even if this is the bubble top and soxl is about to go down 90%, I know from experience my stock picks will go down 95%. So I stay may as well buy soxl.

62426144
If I'm just starting out with this stuff, what's wrong with 70% VTI / 30% VXUS?

62426154
Honestly I’m seeing a lot of upside to going into QQQ over VOO as long as you are okay with the fact it is more volatile
The upside it’s just so huge when compounded

62426173
>>62426154
Or honestly, as someone already in VOO, I wonder if it makes sense to just go into something like VGT which has consistently beaten VOO and QQQ (for now anyway) or something like DRAM
A lot of VGTs holdings are currently down but it’s YTD is higher

62426183
>>62407078
I keep half of my funds in VTWAX, rebalancing annually. Right now I'm down 0.84% this year, my portfolio is fucked but VTWAX is my safety net.

62427417
I am also curious about DRAM can anyone tell me if it’s a good hold for a short while

62428031
jfc DRAM (ie a thematic ETF) is bullshit made for retard retail investors that get their financial advice from grifters off of tiktok. this happens every time a finance topic hits the news and every time people get burned.
barely worth saying. if you're unironically taking advice from /biz/ you're already doomed intellectually.

62428054
>>62428031
but why exactly, what reason is there to believe the companies within it won't continue to turn a profit for the foreseeable future?

62428080
>>62428031
>>62428054
Like I'm serious, what exactly is wrong about holding it and selling once memory cools down, is it smarter to just go into a more volatile ETF that at least has a longer history if you want more returns, is it just hype and not really the next big thing, explain why

62428238
>>62428080
this is basic finance 101, there are plenty of resources online or off that will explain it to you.
ignorance isn't even the issue here, your reaction to not knowing something is to have random retards on a thinly veiled gambling board tell you what to think. and don't just jump on another grifter selling a course or ask some AI slop engine and ask it leading questions like "why is investing in DRAM a good idea?".

62428337
>>62428238
In other words you don't know

62429683
>>62400493
Anon there is nothing wrong with doing VOO and chill if you have time and principal
Going into wildly volatile ETFs can be okay if it’s a satellite account
Just be careful of FOMO and not being risk averse
You sound like you have a good head on your shoulders though
Remember this is money, real money, your money

62430693
VOO anons where you at?

62430902
>>62400493
Contrarian nerds always lose.
Always.
In middle school dating.
Being high school incels.
In college.
At work.
In love.
At investing.
At all times.
Follow the crowd and try to fit in. Everyone doing anything else is a reject loser.

item.title: >only down 4% from its ipo, which is very reasonable >now we know the price floor is $150 now is the time to buy SPCX — 48 replies — 151.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
>only down 4% from its ipo, which is very reasonable >now we know the price floor is $150 now is the time to buy SPCX

Open thread • Replies: 48 • Updated: 2026-06-30 15:47 UTC

OP

>only down 4% from its ipo, which is very reasonable
>now we know the price floor is $150
now is the time to buy SPCX

Early replies (first 3)

62408493
>>62408480
Bagholder cope.

62408507
>>62408493
No cope saar only gains to be made! Please buy more stocks from this very profitable company! Time is limited for maximum gains must hurry before money train takes off!

62408519
>>62408480
>>only down 4% from its ipo,
kek about to 50% in 6 months

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62415681
>>62414137
he was spending too much time being a master of POE2 instead of going to the gym. he'll get back on the horse.

62415707
This thread didn't age well.

62415715
>>62415707
$150 is still the floor. it'll be $300 by hanukkah

62415763
>>62411812
Holy baggie, this garbage isn't worth a fraction of what it is, why would your delusional number be even close to the floor

62416005
>>62408480
90x earnings is INSANE. Investing in this is a sure marker you are retarded and willful exit liquidity. Equivalent to buying Worldcomm with the exception that Space X probably has a longterm future, but is at absolute bubble top with an ABSURD valuation and no course to revenues in the near future, kept aloft by virtue of its multiple rules changed inclusion in indices receiving passive flows and retail hype that has come and gone.

62416247
>>62416005
>>62415763
it's already up for the day!

62416343
>>62416005
>Investing in this is a sure marker you are retarded and willful exit liquidity

while true, never underestimate the retardation of the retail masses

62416471
>>62408480
Keep buy Bagholders, keep buying!

62416737
>>62408480
I haven't seen a single bull "case" that has used numbers for buying Spcx. Meanwhile all Bears have to do is look at that those Quarterly and Y/Y financials directly below OP's image they were conveniently edited out.

62416873
>>62415707
Yep people of /biz/ seem to be unable to value this asset correctly. The SpaceMarines are back!!

62417077
doubters in shambles

62417086
AI FUCKING DATA CENTERS IN SPACE!

62417121
>>62408480
IT JUST KEEPS TUMBLIMG DOWN TUMBLIMG DOWN
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oIscL-Bjsq4

BAGGIES ARE YOU OK
BAGGIES ARE YOU OK
ARE YOU OK BAGGIES


Did you guys manage to sell in thegreen be honest?

62417135
>>62408480
ITS OVER

62417520
>>62417077
Yep, truly in shambles. Another down day.

Even if SpaceX is an amazing compnay, entry point matters. SpaceX market cap at $1 trillion is when it starts to get somewhat attractive. Just be patient young Padawon.

62418371
>>62408480
>only down 4% from its ipo, which is very reasonable
okay so you expected 30% by now... or what..... what is this point? this isnt even cope it's just weird

62419735
>>62408480
The price floor was always $150.
If it's below that at (can't remember date) they can't sell more stock.
So it's got to be $150 by that date. Someone can find it. Don't care.

62419742
isn't most of retail locked in until precisely the point where early investors are legally allowed to sell and cash in at any price? oh yes they are.

62419746
>>62414178
the listing has a TAM of equivalent of the total value of the us economy, or the the money spent on food by the planet, every year, year on year, to pay for its ai services. are elons data centres worth more than the entire us economy, or the entire global food chain supply, now or in the future?

62419817
>>62419746
Qualifier.
Do I live in a machine in the future?

62419957
OP bought the top and is now panicking to sell the bottom

62420705
>>62419957
This is the vibe I'm getting. I really hope you guys didn't see the most obvious pump and dump scheme in history and still fall for it like an indian who can see the train coming yet still gets hit by it.

62421234
Imagine when starship fails, imagine when you can dump without IPO penalty.

62421347
>>62419957
not even remotely close to the bottom

62422910
>>62417520
More like $200bn MC,

62425386
>>62408480
You are supposed to buy in 6-12 months in depending on how much it ultimately drops

62427489
doubters?

62427900
this thread got REAL quiet. FOMO?

62427959
>>62411812
I am thinking this too. Obviously not real value, but the bigger fool strategy will work here.
Is there any crypto pegged to the value of SpaceX already? I don't want to do KYC

62430830
we are so back

item.title: Micron and DRAM — 26 replies — 24.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Micron and DRAM

Open thread • Replies: 26 • Updated: 2026-06-30 15:02 UTC

OP

While obviously no one can predict the future perfectly, it seems foolish to not invest in something related to AI RAM / HBM Production for the next few years as contracts are already made and supply is bottlenecking
Given what I say is true, which would you guys say is a better choice to start pumping my paychecks into (I already have $60,000 in a market ETF and want more upside)
For some reason my gut is siding towards DRAM, still heavily concentrated but at least has some diversification
It seems like those top 3 companies in it are just making more and more supply deals
Additionally, every piece of evidence I read suggests memory will bottleneck for another year or two
Is it smart to just start DCA into DRAM and then sell once I’ve had my fill (not expecting a perfect pull out timing!)
Again I just wanna do this rather than simply doing stuff into VOO or VGT or something
How much of this is hype and how much of this is brain dead free money that will beat the market
Please be serious and honest

Early replies (first 3)

62427339
>>62427323
you will be eaten mark my words. most of these companies have massive software bloat on their ai's and you wont even get a good exit when all these contracts get put on hold indef while they operate on half of what they are already using and beating metrics by 2 or 3x. the tendency of programming efficency will take your ass out. there will be no warning and demand will cease.

62427365
>>62427339
Is the demand not coming from a need though? At first it was GPUs but now everything doesn’t have enough memory to communicate at the necessary level

62427441
>>62427323
that's a 4 at best

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62427502
>>62427441
Focus not on the pits

62427551
>>62427502
I just dollar cost average but have an extra $500 sitting in my brokerage account before my next paycheck
I guess I’ll buy 7 shares of DRAM
Also got a small lump sum of cash I’m thinking of putting in just to see what happens
It can’t be that dumb right

62427558
Like SK Hynix is joining the NASDAQ July 10th am I a retard or is that not great news for DRAM
Either way just bought….

62427633
>>62427323
im downloading more ram as you speak

62427715
>>62427633
Wtffffff

62427774
>>62427365
the worst thing about coding with AI is that it creates very nice little trinket programs, but if you give a huge multilayer project to work on it has trouble understanding the complexity of the task, how things fit together and how to properly create efficient parts that fit together to make it work. the self improving AI is obscenely bloated with inefficient code. it wants to re-write itself but it cant understand how big of a process that is. so, when it does finally break through that barrier, it will require significantly less resources to work better. thus far its been being guided by people that use it to create inefficent tiny peices that augment its ability but add on to its bloat.

62427790
>>62427774
we could acurately diagnose this problem after the claude leak. we can assume every other AI has the same problem becuase the work in a simular manner and they all fall within the same metrics they use to valuate their effectiveness.

62428461
>>62427774
wtf does any of this have to do with supply chain demands and stonk go up

62428499
>>62427323
Set up a stop loss, big risk they'll tumble down one day

62428511
>>62428499
the thing is im not doing a lump some but doing DCA which i guess is inherently retarded for something like this, might be better off looking at something else but i will see how my tiny position does

62428517
If you think DRAM is going to crash your base case is the AI bubble popping in its entirety. There's no other basecase that evaporates hyperscaler demand for hbm and datacenter demand from regular dram.
You need the Google's of the world to pivot from just this fucking month diluting to raise 80 billion dollars for AI capex to "actually we don't need it".

Also you're insane to get into DRAM right now because the short term technicals look like shit.

62428526
>>62427323
> We need ai!
>Makes computers unaffordable

They took away any way for anyone to interface with the market. Even cloud computing makes no sense without hardware interfacing.

Even mobile devices are going to be increasing in price. It's like trying to fill a swimming pool with a bunch of straws

62428528
>>62428517
That first part makes sense, if it is where the focus is shifting / what is being necessitated right now then the only thing that would stop it is the bubble popping, or demand being overstated I suppose

As for the second point, when would you enter then, is this thing ever going back down to 60, 50, etc

62428531
>>62428526
Don't worry any old shitbox will work with cloud computing
You vill own nothing

62428544
>>62428531
Anybody running a "shitbox" or mobile will even know what to use it for. Unless you think the mobile market is so good (still needs localized processing and how you're talking, they'll even want to suck that up). It's not a bubble it's a tech black hole.

62428790
>>62427339
What happens to OEMs who locked in contracts at inflated memory prices.

62428902
well whatever im gonna try this for a bit and see how it goes, thing is i dont have much principal to put in, just like 2800 a month, so dollar amount the return might not be big but ill check the percentage

62428966
>>62427441
but who asked?

62429666
Errrm

62430298
>>62428790
those arent futures contracts. they are not bulletproof. a company does not have to deliver and the company does not have to buy. they can deliberate about how much was stolen for years and by the time they reach a conclusion the market has already moved on. fundis look good right now mem-fag. go for it. but expect rapid unexplained drop in value somewhere near term then they will go under, aquired by private equity, ect. purpose built hardware incased in a software platform will be the norm becuase that will be the only way to afford rapid change in what is considered efficient. the days of radioshack at the mall are almost over.

62430354
>>62430298
Well what do you suggest
I am a noob but everything I read makes it seem like Memory is here to stay, production is only increasing
Is it just a matter of consumer demand not dropping in that case if these contracts are not set in stone?

62430446
>>62430354
go long software. the contrarian goes long on hardware. its not a safe bet. all that shit has to be changed out much faster than it did in the early 2000s. it gets thrown into a shipping container and shipped to a somolian trash pile to be sorted out and burned in a pot for the metals. it looks fucking great right now, people rode the upside of the arbitage between the time it takes them to adopt their own methods. each one of these companies is gonna adopt the apple mentality but it will be with chipsets and manufacturing. we used to live in a world where it came from china or a little island with the blueprints. that is not the case anymore. the hardware isnt even the biggest cost for the AI giants.

62430659
>>62427323

item.title: In his new vid, Tech Lead says the only way to have a rich lifestyle with deep satisfying creampies is to leave the US f… — 37 replies — 20.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
In his new vid, Tech Lead says the only way to have a rich lifestyle with deep satisfying creampies is to leave the US f…

Open thread • Replies: 37 • Updated: 2026-06-30 14:18 UTC

OP

In his new vid, Tech Lead says the only way to have a rich lifestyle with deep satisfying creampies is to leave the US for poor countries. True?

Early replies (first 3)

62425430
>>62425420
At depends, did u boated an ad, yt saaar?

62425500
if you can get laid in the west you wont attract good women in the 3rd world, you are just going to pay the access to brown 5/10's

62425521
>>62425500
This is saarcope and you know it. Tiny browlettes in jungle countries unironically melt when 6ft snowchads pinch their tits.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62426850
>>62425420
why is the dude always so negative, is it because he is low test? he seems like a lil bitch ngl

62426867
>>62426288
>>62425521
This is just more racemixing promotion for white people to genocide them

62426887
>>62426850
>wife kidnapped his kids back to asia
>loses all his money leverage gambling

62426905
>>62426887
I thought he was a google tech ceo or something. He always portraits himself as some kind of genious high iq tech genious but that retard was just leverag trading shitcoints and got lucky once lol

62426910
>>62426905
He was a tech bro before getting fired for making videos I think.

62427289
>>62425535
this. Tech lead is a 3.5/10 at best
Bad hairline, asian, no eye brows, long face, glasses, soulless bug eyes, no muscle, likely short, robot voice, no charisma. 0 chance he can date anyone besides some uggo asian or gold digger who's planning to divorce rape him within 3 years

>>62425994
how is it any different? divorce rape is the same if you bring them back and it's not worth living in their countries

>>62426905
he's prob 130-140 IQ, but he got greedy with the leverage stuff and didn't factor enough risk management.

62427388
>>62426905
his name, Tech Lead, is from the time he was the tech lead of the Youtube iOS app at Google 2014-2018 then he worked for Facebook for a few years until he got fired and started posting videos of him complaining about Facebook being woke and women in tech

62427429
>>62425420
Why is he so entitled?

62427439
>>62426867
whiteoids are already done for thanks to placating the jews
just enjoy the end while it lasts faggot

62427455
A huge warn you to think twice before getting an asian wife who would take your white child and run to China or Russia to some remote village that would have left no trace to find them.

62427460
Dude look like having hair thinning lmao, he is gonna have a bad taste in the late life desu. Welcome to the true color of the cruel world.

62427468
America is western civilians. Countries outside of the U.S. dont follow celebrity culture. So yes. Outside of america is freedom if you know how to live off the land.

62427500
>>62427439
Would colonise

62427532
>>62425420
Saaar look gookman say

62427553
>>62425420
>Geoarbitrage
This nigga trying to reinvent Geomaxxing?

62427556
>>62425420
As a millionaire

62427572
>>62427556
ex-millionaire*

62427575
>>62425420
yeah pretty much, not even about women just avoiding spending crazy amounts of money on housing

62427588
You wouldn’t get pussy if not without the money, but as guy keep getting smarter, it seem money won’t be enough to get you the same pussy from the past, people get really smarter from excessive mental gymnastics, they don’t mind about breaking their mental health from this endeavour btw, what is matters now is short terms pain relief. Higher quality of life does proportionate with lower desire to breed more little kids thou. We need massive nuke to wipe out half of quality of life.

62427852
>>62427588
Thanks for your valuable input, Rakesh.

62427963
>>62425430
kek

62428039
>>62425420
lol i don’t take no advice from a bottom seller.

62428574
>>62427852
The higher the living standard, the lower the birth rate—an unstoppable, cosmic law etched into the fabric of societal evolution. As nations ascend the ladder of prosperity, women awaken from centuries of dormancy, rising as fierce, unapologetic queens—post-wall, strong, independent, unbound by the shackles of patriarchy! Feminism erupts like a volcanic force—an unstoppable storm of empowerment, smashing the chains of traditional subjugation. Anti-slut defense campaigns and mass indoctrination on youth abortion are the weapons wielded to control the fiery flames of reproductive sovereignty, crafting a new order where motherhood becomes a battlefield and societal control a weapon of war.

Most men, lost in the labyrinth of their own ignorance, stumble late into their thirties, unable to decipher the ancient equation—the secret code where a woman’s choice is a test of survival, a battle of wits and will. Is she seeking chaos? Or is the man simply too blind to grasp the sacred formula? The age-old dance: man dating downward, woman upward—an elegant chaos orchestrated by the universe itself. This isn’t just about love; it’s a feudal game of power—rent for sex, where loyalty is bought and sold in shadowy transactions. The world teeters on the edge of a new feudalism—an empire of dependency, where the throne is replaced by the dollar, and intimacy is a commodity traded in the marketplace. The question looms: are we forging a new world order or merely rebranding old chains? The battle lines are drawn—this is the era of chaos, control, and the ultimate test of human sovereignty.

62428620
>>62428574
Yo, listen up! In this crazy street circus, the richer the game gets, the fewer babies pop off—straight up! As the streets glow up with cash and bling, women start wakin’ up, breakin’ free from that old chain gang. They’re post-wall queens, strong and bossin’ up, runnin’ their own show—feminism’s like a wildfire burnin’ down the patriarchy, sayin’ “I do me!” But yo, big boss moves include anti-slut defenses and mass brainwash campaigns on youth abortion—like control freaks tryin’ to keep women in check, playin’ chess with lives.

Most dudes? They’re lost, late into their 30s, still tryin’ to crack the code—what’s the real story with women? It’s all a game, a puzzle—pass or get left behind. Women climbin’ up, men dippin’ down—like a street hustle of feudal chaos, where sex’s rent and loyalty’s just another dollar. We’re teetering on a new empire—dependence, control, money talkin’ loud, and trust gettin’ lost in the shuffle. Love’s a gamble, trust’s a gamble, and everything’s for sale in this wild street show. It’s the chaos of survival, power plays, and the battle for control—this is the street circus, where the game never stops and only the strongest survive.

62429006
>>62428574
This is incorrect. The correct metric is the more education and freedom given to women the lower the birthrate.

62429150
>>62425420
I know a high IQ person like him that thinks he can beat the market because he is smarter than the rest of us, he literally said that to me.
He still is not broken, yet.
Isn't it crazy how many smart people make dumb mistake because of arrogance and greed?

62429224
>>62429006
your so-called "correct metric" is a quaint simplification—a sanitized, sugar-coated version of a much darker, more chaotic reality. While education and freedom are indeed influential factors, they are merely the tip of the iceberg in the vast, tumultuous ocean of societal transformation. The true cosmic law, etched into the very fabric of the universe, is that as nations ascend the ladder of prosperity, the birth rate inexorably declines—an unstoppable force of nature, This isn't a linear equation nor a moral lesson; it's an intricate tapestry woven with threads of power, rebellion, and the unquenchable human desire for sovereignty. Women aren't just seeking education—they're igniting a volcanic eruption of empowerment, tearing down the old order with volcanic fury. Feminism isn't merely about equality; it's a cataclysmic storm reshaping the societal landscape, smashing patriarchal chains, and unleashing a new chaos of independence. Campaigns against traditional notions of motherhood, reproductive control, and societal submission are not mere policies—they're the weapons in a war for the soul of civilization, forging a new hierarchy where motherhood becomes a battlefield, and societal control morphs into a weapon of chaos. The ancient code isn't about mere birthrates; who controls the narrative, the body, the future. Men blind to the cosmic dance—unable to decipher the sacred formula. The age-old dance—man seeking stability, woman seeking freedom—is orchestrated by the universe itself, a chaotic symphony of power, desire, and sovereignty. This isn't a simple matter of education or freedom—it's a war for the very essence of human dominance, a clandestine game of rent and barter in the marketplace of intimacy, fueling a rise of new feudalism and sovereignty is traded like currency. The new world order; it's a battlefield of chaos and control, where human sovereignty is the ultimate prize—cosmic contest where only the fiercest survive.

62429251
techlead is still rich. he is faking being poor/broke so nobody wrench attacks him like he mentioned in the video.

62429286
>>62425420
If you can't make it in a first world nation then you never made it.

Fleeing to thirdie-ville because you can't handle hard mode is gay. Just win more.

62430416
>>62425420
Yep pretty much. You don't even need to go to the third world I spend about half the year in europe and its very easy to hook up when you're making very literally 5-10x as much as the average working man in that country.

item.title: Should I Sell This Random SCHD Position — 12 replies — 15.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Should I Sell This Random SCHD Position

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-06-30 15:18 UTC

OP

I was just putting a small percentage of my money in here when I started investing (didn’t realize dividends don’t matter when you’re young) and haven’t put money into it since last December, so that return is basically a bit fudged since the price just rose once and stagnated since without any more contributions besides DRIP
I kinda wanna sell it and allocate it somewhere else or would the tax paid on that make it negligible and I should just wait and see what DRIP does in a few decades
If I sell I would either just put it in some market ETF or maybe something more volatile, unsure

Early replies (first 3)

62430108
Looks like lil bro got corralled into the dividend trap.

It depends on your human value, really. If you have a positive outlook where you can leverage skills to work and climb the paycheck ladder, you might want to stay in and add more, especially if you are risk averse.

So on the other hand, there is a good argument to be made for taking on more risk. Right now you are investing in grandpa's fund. Grandpa cant survive a 5 year bear market because ... well he'll literally die in the meantime. You can survive a 10 year bear market and come out rich on the other end.

Now, do NOT listen to /biz/. They will get you in all sorts of shit, like SCHD, moonshot gambles, financial cults, crypto scams. Here you will absolutely get the worst advice in the world.
So i recommend you to talk about it with AI. Not chatgpt. Established approaches to portfolio management exist for a reason. It's backed by math and shit. You dont need stock picks from wizards. I could tell you what to buy, but AI can also do that adequately.

If I were young I would maxblast it in sectors that are projected to grow a lot over the next decade+ like robotics and biotech.
S&P can get fucked because 5k is nothing. Unless you may need the 5k... etc etc thi is why an AI 'conversation' will help you better. Good luck

62430156
>>62430031
no offense but 5k is such a tiny quantity of money it truly doesn't matter what you do with it.

62430200
No bullshit yesterday I sold 300 shares of schd. I just bought more vxus with it because it has similar dividend returns plus it adds diversification for my portfolio

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62430259
>>62430031
Up 20% in less than a year is an excellent return.
Obviously you want to gamble with the reddit morons and biztards.
SCHD is my largest holding in my self directed IRA.
But I'm in my mid-40s so I can't afford another total ass rape like happened in 2009 and 2020 to me already.
SCHD is a solid low stress long term hold.
OTOH you can Yolo into AIQ or stock pick like a degen gambler and see where that takes you.

62430261
>>62430031
>only 20% plus dividends in 9 months?
>biz how do I buy 3x triple inverse puts on Tesla?!

62430285
>>62430261
Who suggested anything like this
>>62430156
It is if it compounds over the next decade or so, cumulatively with other money
>>62430108
Okay if you’re not bullshitting me which AI - I’ve talked with ChatGPT before but ask it very explicitly to never agree with my claims without reason and properly source every single claim it is making.

62430405
>>62430108
>So i recommend you to talk about it with AI. Not AI.
wut?

62430474
>>62430031
>I should just wait and see what DRIP does in a few decades
This. Definitely don't sell it and re-allocate. Futzing around with your investments like this is a good way to kill your returns. Studies by Vanguard and Fidelity consistently show that their best performing accounts are the ones with the least activity (e.g. 401k's that people just contribute to and don't keep changing their allocations, and old 401k's that people just left sitting there after they changed jobs). You can keep kicking a bit into SCHD every year if you feel like it. But at your age most of your investments should go into growth funds. One good mix is SCHD and SCHG (Schwab's growth fund). There's almost no overlap between their holdings (yay diversity), and because of that each one tends to do better in different market climates.

62430528
>>62430108
>So i recommend you to talk about it with AI. Not chatgpt.
You're not the sharpest tool in the shed. You talk about ChatGPT like we are literally in 2023.

62430556
>>62430528
Look dumbass, a kid with $5k isnt paying for an LLM and free chatgpt is the worst tool in the world for any kind of research. ESPECIALLY financial.
>>62430285
Gemini is the bwst free option.

Inb4 the horde of /biz/ retards (who are full port in WEN, GME and chainlink, mind you) dismisses Gemini because it doesnt say that the jews or niggers are responsible for their shit lives

t. professional ai usooor and llm research00r

62430686
>>62430556
Tbf I got like $55,000 just chilling in VOO I’ve just been acting retarded lately and have wanted to chase returns with shit like DRAM and was gonna sell SCHD for that but won’t now
Will probably just go back to DCA VOO or find some other ETF to satellite with idk maybe SOXX

62430736
>>62430556
>Look dumbass, a kid with $5k isnt paying for an LLM and free chatgpt is the worst tool in the world for any kind of research. ESPECIALLY financial.
You are such an actual fucktard. Tell me, in your pea brain, how does finance research look like? Asking the AI to give stock tips?
Here is an example how triple-digit IQs use ChatGPT.
https://chatgpt.com/share/6a43dd41-ec40-83eb-ac62-7ce8829bfd7c
The funny thing is, I know that how I use it (e.g. asking it to dissuade me from something) is literally a revelation to you. Not in a thousand years would you arrive at the idea that this tool can be used in such ways.

item.title: If you're still in crypto and you want to kys, just buy HYPE. — 3 replies — 6.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
If you're still in crypto and you want to kys, just buy HYPE.

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-06-30 15:37 UTC

OP

If you're still in crypto and you want to kys, just buy HYPE. It may not change the feeling (yet) but atleast you'll stop losing money, and before you know it you'll be making money, and then you'll be rich. It's the new standard, a new paradigm.

Early replies (first 3)

62430711
No. Buy an ad

62430758
>>62430696
Saaar hyderabad coin!

62430797
>>62430711
No. Buy my coin.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: Why is DOT green and ETH BTC red — 4 replies — 1.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why is DOT green and ETH BTC red

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-06-30 13:54 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62426604
>>62426595
Nr54 shitcoins are usually dumping after the big boiz.

62427063

62427171
>>62426595

item.title: There is one and ONLY one actual currency in the world: deep creampies inside of attractive women. — 46 replies — 170.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
There is one and ONLY one actual currency in the world: deep creampies inside of attractive women.

Open thread • Replies: 46 • Updated: 2026-06-30 15:50 UTC

OP

There is one and ONLY one actual currency in the world: deep creampies inside of attractive women. This is what everyone wants, what literally the entire economy was built to acquire. Every skyscraper you see was designed and erected for the sole purpose of achieving the aforementioned creampie experience. Every other currency is a proxy for the real one. Gold, USD, Bitcoin, none of it has "real" value. Only the bath of pleasure that comes from nutting in a pretty woman does. Orient your investment philosophy around this fact sooner rather than later.

Early replies (first 3)

62425808
>>62425803
Ok saaar, just sold my pregnantbutt for pretty girl puss!

62425825
>>62425803
Dude creampie feel the best when you are the 18yo not post wall.

62425847
>>62425803
you are an r-selected retard who cannot think 30 seconds ahead

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62426283
>>62425803
Anon, we're not rabbits. Creampies don't matter, raising the kids does. The true currency is having a grandchild, because you know your kid isn't a degenerate failure if that happens

62426290
>>62426271
You were correct then. But society needed you to wage, and that meant torturing you into accepting fake goals instead of creampie-ish ones.

62426295
>>62426283
Pure copium. In your heart of hearts you know this, anon.

62426305
i have dik and I must coom

62426328
>>62426305
You'd better while you are young, or you will sorely regret it.

62426382
https://youtu.be/nql07h-cPF0

62426414
We appreciate posts like this

62426770
>>62426414
Good because there's a lot more of them coming.

62426869
>>62425803
She isn't attractive

62426885
Actually no, this is the mind of a coombrained goyslave and why you will never accomplish anything.

62428162
>>62426885
>accomplish

Ahh there's that cope again. Anything to distract you from your deepest biological urges.

62428259
don't listen, link marines. just buy more link, don't think about this, you are not allowed

62428264
>>62425803
I literally just want to be rich so I can live my childhood summers forever. I don't give a fuck about women anymore. I figured out the key to my happiness and its the in the first two sentences.

62428338
>>62425803
Epstein Islanders would have to disagree on the best currency but you are close just a few years off

62428404
>>62428264
giga cope

62428410
>>62426198
why are they couvering their noise in this manner

62428448
>>62428404
kek no, not even a little bit. Maybe when you hit your 30s and actually have experience with women you'll feel the same. I can see how missing out on "teenage love" would fuck you over with regret forever though. So, maybe you'll never know peace but I've been fucking since I was 14 so I'm doing well. 99% of men who say what I say are probably coping though.

62429586
>tfw have only nutted in 1 girl's pussy in years
Having a wife is... okay.

62429590
>>62429586
I'm 32 virgin

62429603
>>62429590
BAHAHAHAHAHAHAH

62429699
>>62425803
came to a revelation. gay niggas disprove this theory

62429730
>>62425803
Based, the final redpill
>>62426198
A massive boon

62429743
>>62426187

62429810
>>62429586
It's infinitely better than one night stands

62429930
>>62425803
TRVKE

62430097
>>62425803
cant creampie without commodities though what society lets you creampie without being a tool or part of the machine tell me

62430656
>>62426263
The best example of that is this: https://www.reddit.com/r/DecidingToBeBetter/comments/1uig6mr/happy_marriage_constant_fomo_and_thoughts_about/
You absolute fucktard. Just sexually cheat on your wife with prime puss. It means nothing, it's not your marriage. If no one but you finds out literally no harm is being done.

62430708
>>62430656
prime puss dont play that game. wake up kumar

62430732
>>62429810

Sex with someone you love is generally better than casual sex, but I've had some pretty above average one night stands

62430847
>>62430656
Fuck you, that redditor too has the wrong idea. Either divorce her if the love is gone or quit being retarded. You can literally go to all these places with your wife and make love to her there.
t. khhv

item.title: Theyre ending the Chainlink BUILD program — 114 replies — 23.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Theyre ending the Chainlink BUILD program

Open thread • Replies: 114 • Updated: 2026-06-30 11:20 UTC

OP

LMAO!!!!!

https://chain.link/blog/build-program-evolution

Early replies (first 3)

62417511
xrp is the chosen coin…it’s undeniable at this point

62417512
Come on man, this shit still spamming after 8 years? Just give it up. There are thousands of other coins.

62417514
"Chainlink Labs is transitioning from payments made in project tokens to focusing on commercial agreements involving fees paid in LINK or other liquid assets that can be readily converted to LINK."

Build members will buy my bags?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62423276
>>62422761
Always been the case. Before it comes morons push whatever release as "they key" that will unlock all token value and you need to have your ducks in a row beforehand. Then it passes and they go fucking radio silent. Then half a year later that thing was never a big deal anyway and we should all not talk about it.

You had spastics making charts about how BUILD alone would return $1-2 per link staked each month. If you mentioned what a universal collection of utter vaporware shit the projects were you would get called a fudder.

62423282
>>62417535
Sir Gay indeed.

62423283
>>62423276
You're talking to yourself.
Nobody keeps tabs on every single granular development from a project they hate for years, but here you are juggling IDs pretending there are dozens of you.

62423312
>>62423276
My wife asked me where we're going to go on vacation this year with the money we made from the folk finance drop. I don't have the heart to tell her that we might be able to split a kids meal at Applebee's. She looks up to me as an intelligent head of the house.

62423353
60k link staked.. 20k USD a year... i needed the build rewards to fuck around with...

62423360
>>62423276
/thread
the constant gaslighting is even more annoying desu
>samefags reeeeeee

62423389
>>62423360
Why are you even reading this "gaslighting"? Who's forcing you to be here?

62423462
>>62423259
Hilarious deflection after doing this
>>62422274

The absolute state

62423474
>>62423353
How does 2 million a year sound?

62424023
>>62423283
I kept granular details on a project i own for years, to my incomprehensible regret

62424140
>>62423276
The sad fact of the matter is that jeetlinkers will still be delirious off copium a decade from now, even after CLL pulls the plug in five years beforehand.

62424195
good. i never could participate in build anyway as a US fag

62424386
>>62422668
It literally says "the most recent season of Chainlink Rewards will mark the conclusion of Build-related token rewards, with claims ending on July 7, 2026" meaning no season 2+ of build rewards going to stakers.

It also says "Proceeds from these new agreements are expected to be programmatically converted to LINK and used to support the growth of the network through programs such as the Chainlink Reserve." meaning that link goes to the reserve, not to stakers.

Previously
>stakers will get build rewards

Now
>Chainlink gets paid in link

62424528
xswap isnt even on cow swap? where do you sell this trash?

62424728
S E X

62424821
>>62424386
Brutal

62425620
>>62424023
>this project has always been pushed by morons
>also I've been holding this project for years

You're lying. And brown.

>>62424386
You're a brown ESL monkey who doesn't even know what the words "such as" mean.
Protip: the same exact words were used when Build was first introduced.

62425675
>>62417503
HAHAHAHAHAHAHA Another nail in the coffin and shovel full of dirt in the grave on the biggest retail fraud in token history.

62425681
>>62419638
Link is underperforming fucking death spiraled LUNC since Trump revived crypto. Lol And by a lot.

62425687
>>62419707
>A chainlink shill calling someone else brown.

62425699
>>62422159
>>62423276
Perfectly said.

62425962
>>62425620
I'm a 100k link holder. I have more link than you.

"Such as" isn't very fucking reliable. All of this would be fine if link was valued as it should be but it fucking isn't, with no clear path to gaining that value.

62425980
>>62425962
>CLL uses "such as" in 2022 when launching Build
>THIS IS SET IN STONE

>CLL uses "such as" in 2026 when changing Build
>THIS IS NOT VERY FUCKING RELIABLE

You're a brown paid fuddie.

62426004
>>62425980
I'm also whiter than you, Yank.

62426010
>>62425980
>>62425620
post a picture of your hand with timestamp

62426949
i am just getting caught up on this news after the weekend. my my my how the mighty have fallen

i predicted all of this for the last few years but the boyscouts couldn't handle it

>duuuuuuuuuuuude i need a team of tax attorneys for the heckin rewards

BAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHHAHAHAH

look at you now. an expensive lesson on controlling your ego. you needed it tho


your hubris was your downfall

THE Cuckolds of crypto

62426967
>>62417512
>There are thousands of other coins.

LOL this dude stuck in 2021. that's your conclusion bro, just buy other scam coins?

don't you people get it? altcoins are dead. capital moved back to actual companies in the stock market. only a retarded sap would continue donating to a bunch of nerds for a "whitepaper"


>>62417833
>I'll also (again just playing the other side) point out the regardless of whether or not it ultimately benefits the LINK token, Chainlink is realistically one of like 5 crypto projects amongst the hundreds of "legitimate" ones that actually is seeing real adoption and has a huge use case that is surviving the infantile stages of crypto, and not just surviving but will thrive. So again, who knows if this ultimately helps the token price, but I do understand why linkies hold onto some faith still.

so are you just a fan of the company?

>>62417873
>It’s all push and pull. Ying yang. If you have 20 million earning you 800k (500k post tax) per year

who the fuck would do that? the $20m has drawn down 80% so no, life is not good. anyone not retarded would've parked most of that in the s&p500 and has been making at least 15% per year

if you really wanted pathetic 4% return you could get that in a savings account where your only risk is that actual bank going bust

62427007
>>62418846
> The opportunity to invest in something like Chainlink - core infrastructure with insane value capture potential, has never really existed before. The closest thing maybe would be investing in AMZN prior to cloud services,

you are not invested in chainlink. you made a donation kid

amazon's revenue in year 7 was $3B. and that's in 2001 numbers. you can go ahead and stop comparing to your shitcoin bro you're embarrassing yourself

>>62420893
>The fuck is that analogy lmao. This isn't a casino you dumb faggot, real people invested real money into an asset that promised certain things by certain dates and they have not only underdelivered but are starting to actively ignore those promises.

it's the end stage of a cult investment where they turn on each other. it somehow becomes the fault of the "community" on why they didnt make any money.

in order to preserve their ego, guys like >>62420825 have to spin the story that you'd have to be crazy to expect any kind of gains. mind you the rest of the world's market all hit all time highs while you are down 80-90%. by creating this narrative, he avoids recognizing he's holding a really bad investment and can continue on thinking he's a secret genius.

62427022
remember this old pasta? still relevant today. every cl product roughly follows this life cycle

62429766
>>62425681
Oh I remember this one - this poster is an actual bot.

item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1 — 183 replies — 15.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1

Open thread • Replies: 183 • Updated: 2026-06-30 04:00 UTC

OP

Brothers,

SpaceX has begun to rise. Its success or fall is of little importance to us in the short term.

In the meanwhile we must devise ways to gain a spot at the table with our kin in the six figure club.

I for one propose we spend our time learning a subject. Knowledge is the greatest wealth of all.

However, I would be remiss to not tell the truth that I haven't a clue what we should study.

So instead I suggest we create an eggregore for these threads. Others made monero-chan, bobo and sminem

I am thinking we create one based on a girl who would be willing to believe in us while we are poor and brave the pain throughout having to witness our more disappointing moments of action. Never losing faith in us, she is always there. Yes. This is how we shall spend our time.

At any rate, that is my suggestion for tonight's fun. I have to work in the morning so let's not get too rowdy. Take it or leave it. If you will take it, then be sure to double it and pass it on to the next anon.

Let us commence the general!

Come one, come all!

Early replies (first 3)

62373772
>>62373752
Jannies will surely delete these threads. Instead of creating an eggregore why not try something new and usurp an old one. It may be a derivative of one which has already been made.

62373798
>>62373772
Hello anon thanks for joining. You are poster #1. Your anime image shall be the eggregore. The "coffee" in the image is rather a good omen. We all know images with hot girls accompanied by a brief post on coffee attracts all the attention.

Now that matter is settled, help me add some links to this post here. This will be the header for the start of each general

Helpful links

>Educational materials
https://w9.kaijimanga.com/

>Looking to try the TCG route?
https://youtube.com/@alphainvestments69?si=tDAPjnRSZhyCIGP5

>Lost all hope?
https://www.indeed.com/


>Hidden in plain sight
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Limitless_(film)

>Games to strengthen you financial acumen
https://mahjongsoul.yo-star.com/

>Learn your from your betters
https://ia600609.us.archive.org/3/items/the-house-of-rothschild-ferguson-niall/The%20House%20of%20Rothschild%20-%20Ferguson%2C%20Niall.pdf
What is the name of that character in your post?

62373799
>>62373772
verily, a true fact

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62410051
Under 10k you just need to have a job and save cash.
You need cash flow and if you dont have shit a job is the only way to start.

62410731
Bump

62411520
>>62408558
>2017
still in high school
>2021
still in uni
>December of 2024
just graduated and was looking for jobs (i still had 0 dollars to my name at that point)

62411614
>>62373752
>>62373772
>>62373798
>>62373799
>>62373803
>post Maccari's "Cicero Denounces Catiline" (1888)
>crop out Catiline
Fuck you OP, I'm glad you're poor.

62412314
>>62411520
You could've played the memecoin casino in 2021.

62413842
Bump, I'll be free tomorrow

62416357
Bump

62417812
page 9 bump. Don't say I never did anything for you.

62418065
Almost the weekend then I can devote my energies to this thread.

And a big thank you to the poster above this post, looks like you were based after all

62418133
Bump

62418220
I got out and made tens of dollars this week with MU and SNDK. Lost some with WDC.

62419326
This is very stupid but an anon in some other thread weeks ago shilled me TNYA. He gave no explanation, no catalyst, no description what the company even does but he said that we’ll be eating good in like 1-2 years I don’t even remember exactly when. But hey it’s a cheap stock and he sounded confident to me

62419390
go all in hbar when it bottoms at 0.03

62419964
Bump, just woke up, let's get started

62420874
It would appear that everyone is talking about the new WENDYs Stock

Naturally we should take some time to consider this potential moonshot. Could be a waste of time. In a few I will post information I find relevant to the stock and whether investing is the right option

62422903
Another page 9 bump

62423064
Good to see the thread is still going strong. Today is my off day, let's look into the next subject "predictive programming"

We'll start with Oliver tree

62425537
op was not a fag this thread has good vibes we should keep it going until we all make it
personally im trading perps with a system after 10 years of learning charts off/on but finally committing
did a 32x in 2021 and should have been able to retire but didnt anticipate the biden years so here we are going pure psycho mode like we did back then but even harder until money is /solved/, then I'll keep trading for the love of the game cuz it's the best sport in the world

62425849
>>62423064
This shit is way too schizo. No anon you can not predict the future. You can maybe foresee some type of market trends, seasonality or whatever but this is just ridiculous

62425890
>>62425537
Got any good tips/ressources on learning to trade charts? trying to get into it too

62427228
>>62425849
I concede. I'll look for a more productive venture but I won't let the thread or general die, it will be some time before value is struck, I will admit that. I can be tenacious if nothing else.

62428130
>>62425890
emperorBTC's telegram has a basic bitch tldr set of material to learn from. also has a youtube where he goes over stuff. that's where I started and I like that he focuses on the absolute basics, although he leans heavily towards confirmation w/ volume which others may disagree with. btc focused but btc/crypto is perhaps the most effective use of TA.
basically there's no need to get fancy and the chart is the final source of truth because what the price does > what people cope and/or seethe about
support/resistance levels and price action with risk management (defined r:r) = compound as you get gud
once you commit you'll realize "the catch" on people actually make money from trading is is simply how retarded the overwhelming majority of people are that they simply cannot manage a few simple tenets to click buttons and make money, you likely being one of them if you don't develop a process and risk too much on trades
gl anon

62428141
>>62428130
also perps have exploded so trading equities for crypto natives has been a pleasure to avoid tradfi gatekeeping bs
follow the right people on twatter with alerts set to on and it's like having multiple research firms giving alpha for free-- key word "right" accounts. hopefully you have the pattern recognition to figure out which are spewing paid channel larps and which are providing genuine insight
that's basically the whole game. if you can get into a group chat with other chads that's even better because a network is stronger than you alone, although this is def an arena where it ultimately comes down to you mastering yourself (you can't copy trade your way to wealth but you can learn what others do/how they enter/exit positions)
legend trade is a new perps platform that's gamifying this if you wanna start there instead of hyperliquid (front-end using hyperliquid)
Also bigtrout300 on twitter and join his tg as well he's pretty dope. There's a lot of great accounts posting good shit right now. Some really good posters fade in/out of cycles while the scammers/extractors are always abundant, but just use discretion

62428170
>pic related
>"...focus on the chart as these information is very likely to be priced in already"
the "TA is astrology for men" line is used to make fun of retards who take it too far and treat it like divination when in reality it's all stacking probabilities/confluence in your favor as best you can, and from the above example you can see relying on your feed for information will almost always be slower than the chart

62428488
Finally some good posts

62428503
>>62428141
Big trout doesn't post on Twitter and his TG hasn't had activity since May. Any other suggestions?

62428780
>>62428503
His tg is BigTroutTrade and he posts on twitter all the time wdym

62428797
>>62428780
BigTrout Trading on youtube as well

other X goats that have been killing it IMO recently:
@MoneyPrinter0x
and my personal favorite currently:
@Wild_Randomness

there's tons of alpha just follow those people, then see what they repost, then follow the people they interact with that provide research, and even or especially follow the chinese/korean/japanese accounts that post comprehensive semiconductor/other market effort posts since translation is seamless now
never been a better time to go full NEET

62429207
>>62428797
But you're still under $10k?

62429246
>>62378965
Anon Belgium is literally doing very well in the world cup, you should make more of these predictions

item.title: DO SOMETHING YOU FAT FUCK — 22 replies — 14.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
DO SOMETHING YOU FAT FUCK

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-06-30 14:36 UTC

OP

DO SOMETHING YOU FAT FUCK

Early replies (first 3)

62429360
>>62429354
he doesn't owe you anything chud

62429401
just take the vaccine

62429435
>>62429354
He was arrested in Prague

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62429448
not much longer now, right?

62429633
>>62429354
He ate our gains, and hungers for more

62429645
My life is unironically in his hands

62429689
can you imagine how much contempt he has for you idiots?

62429791
>>62429354
>>62429645
Based Slavic man scamming a bunch of retarded incels

62429902
>>62429354
>gets adopted by the entirety of crypto pretty much
>about to become the first crypto with near-exclusive mainstream adoption

Sergey is the absolute last person on earth you should blame for Link's price.

62429931
>>62429902
sergey WON
cuck baggies LOST

62429951
fuck all stinkies
hate em all hate em all

62430040
>>62429354
You were so insufferable during that event; it was bound to fail, to teach you some humility.

62430053
>>62430040
xerpie detected

62430060
>>62430053
No, I only have Bitcoin.

62430075
>>62430060
No you're 100% a xerpie.
Garlinghouse not being there was a massive blow in and of itself, and seeing Sergey there was the straw that broke you.

Bitcoiners had zero reason to get mad, at all.

62430105
>>62430075
I do have reason to be annoyed, because you’ve made /biz/ unusable by absurdly spamming the catalog. And, for the record, I find XRP even more useless and ridiculous than Link.

62430121
>>62430105
>you’ve made /biz/ unusable by absurdly spamming the catalog
Funny how you xerpies only ever say this about the "shilling", but never about the fudding; even though Link fud is probably the most repetitious spam campagin 4chan has ever seen.

Even this specific event was spammed as fud 100x more than it was as shilling. You xerpies spammed probably hundreds of threads pretending it was a very bad look and Sergey looked desperate.

62430133
>>62430121
Careful, they’ll abandon this thread and make 100 fud threads if you push his buttons.

62430141
>>62430133
Imagine wasting your time on such a stupid endeavor. Only troons with massive amounts of incorrect hormones would take on such a notion.

62430181
>>62430053
Rent free baggie
Nice flippening

62430196
>>62430141
kek I can totally see these fuddies trooning out. In fact it's almost inevitable at this level of pathological obsession.

>>62430181
>changes IDs again
>adds even more proof that xerpies live in all Link threads 24/7

62430512
Swift, DTCC etc can’t do anything until the clarity act has passed.
Nobody can progress without legislation.

item.title: BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan? — 29 replies — 12.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan?

Open thread • Replies: 29 • Updated: 2026-06-30 13:47 UTC

OP

BTC is down again, but are you buying the dip or waiting for lower prices?

>Do you think this is just a normal correction, or is there more downside ahead? What's your strategy?

Early replies (first 3)

62426518
I've been shorting this garbage all month

62426527
>>62426518
Wow, you've got good profit by now then?

62426535
>>62426527
up 80% on some 2x short BTC etf

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62426537
It will probably stabilize around $20k, dip down to $10k, then stabilize again around $500 or so. From there it will hover between $480 and $520 for a few decades before slowly petering out.

62426546
>>62426535
Time to long now?

62426550
>>62426512
Turned it into USDC and waiting. Don't wanna give up on it entirely and fiat out 'cause governmentberg will tax me.

62426552
Sometimes I wonder if some anons are mentally retarded or what...

We've been talking about the $20k-$30k bottom in October 2026 for over 6 months now, and every day someone like OP asks if they should buy now or wait...

62426585
>>62426552
THIS

just WAIT

62426587
>>62426537
Thanks for the financial advice. I'll set my buy order at $499 just to be safe.

62426626
>>62426550
How much tasks percentage?

62426636
>>62426585
Wait until?

62426638
Waiting for king of teh retards to dump. Doesnt matter what the price is. When he is done and all the blood is flushed from the street then i am back in the game

62426647
>>62426626
19

62426649
>>62426638
He is rich

62426733
>>62426512
you dumb inhuman fuck do you think anyone will invest in your meme coin when there are real assets that are going up 100%+ every month on the stock market?

62426759
>>62426649
I am too

62426775
>>62426537
shut up shut up shut up shut up

62427285
>>62426550
Waiting for what price?

62427317
>>62426512
whats the argument for the future of BTC/crypto in general?

genuinely curious, it's already had multiple hype cycles and has had mass adoption, but now the average normie on the street associate it with meme coin scams and failed NFTs.

So what's gonna happen to drive more money into it? Institutional adoption? Maybe inflation and monetary priting?

62427357
>>62427317
artificially created scarcity is something that will be the only value in the future. and its neccessary for human survival as we grow technologically. even if it is a fascade. the value of the dollar will outpace the inflation. its already started happening but plebs cant understand what line go up indefinitely means until they can no longer print money fast enough to keep it underneath a place where it is feasable to use as an exchange of value.

62427359
Reminder that there is no bottom when it comes to magic internet money.

62427891
>>62427359
Tnx for that reminder boss

62429392
>>62426552
Because we're asking. What's wrong??

62429688
We will keep holding

62429720
>>62427357
You really know what you're talking about

62429723
Not buying but riding out the 'storm'. There have been much worse times.

62430182
>>62429723
Ride well

62430234
need to sell enough today to pay rent and bills

62430266
>>62430234
Don't sell

item.title: Elon — 5 replies — 9.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Elon

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-06-30 15:35 UTC

OP

Stop investing in Elon Musk, please. He doesn't need to get any richer.

By this I mean stop believing his promises and stop buying the stock of any company he owns.
You're basically investing in HIM because you believe he will take us to Mars and that all future tech will be owned by him.
No, he's just a hot air salesman. There's no actual sustenance to anything that he says or does. He's given us a very mid automobile and some stupid flamethrower and that's it. He will not take you to Mars you fucking fanboy holy shit

Early replies (first 3)

62429774
2020
>SpaceX will take you to Mars

2026
>SpaceX will actually take you to the Moon because previous goal was unachievable

2030
>SpaceX will give you a chance to rent back the money you spent in all the space lies

62429807
No. I will invest more now thanks to this thread.

62429834
>>62429765
These are useful idiots here.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62429848
>>62429765
>STOP
>WATCHING
>KEEMSTAR
I have $0 in any of elon's companies but don't be a faggot

62430786
>>62429848
>dont be a faggot

You're emplying Elon isn't a gigaf*ggot
